params.name: Xiuhtecuhtli Mask
params.description:
On Mar 1, 1466 AD, the Xiuhtecuhtli mask was gifted to a priest for a religious ritual.
This turquoise mask represents Xiuhtecuhtli, the Aztec god of fire. This valuable mask is made by laminating thin sheets of turquoise with wood in a unique patch process. Xiuhtecuhtli, whose name means Lord of Turquoise, was widely worshiped. In the ancient Aztec calendar Tonalpohualli, Xiuhtecuhtli rules the trecena Atl.
